title: Week of March 23, 2026
summary: This week brought major improvements to Windows users with a streamlined setup process and native installation support. We enhanced Salesforce operations with better revenue field detection, deployment safeguards, and smoother permission handling. Security was strengthened with automated secret scanning, and the platform now handles production environments more safely with improved read-only query support.

entries.headline: Improved safety for Salesforce data retrieval operations
details: Data retrieval commands now run safely without triggering production warnings
Better protection policies prevent accidental changes to live systems

entries.headline: Automated release notes system now generates weekly updates
details: Release notes automatically compile and publish from development changes
Updates deploy daily to keep you informed of the latest improvements

entries.headline: Salesforce permission management works more reliably
Resolved six cases where operations were blocked unnecessarily
details: Added bypass options for legitimate administrative tasks
Narrowed overly strict rules that prevented valid operations
Improved detection of safe versus risky commands

entries.headline: Simple record lookups now work without extra routing steps
details: Single record queries by ID run directly for faster results
Short verification queries skip unnecessary complexity
Bounded result queries under 5 records streamlined

entries.headline: Windows setup now installs required tools automatically
details: Claude Code installs natively on Windows systems
Node.js downloads and configures automatically
Streamlined installation reduces manual setup steps

entries.headline: Fixed Windows installation errors with search results
System now detects and reports connection problems automatically
details: Identifies authentication issues with Salesforce orgs
Provides helpful context when operations fail
Validates connections before starting complex tasks

entries.headline: PowerShell installation script works with one-line commands
details: Fixed syntax issues when running install commands from web
Prevents PowerShell window from closing unexpectedly
Improved error handling during automated installation

entries.headline: Read-only queries now work safely on production Salesforce orgs
Revenue data context now saves properly in assessment reports
New customization system lets you safely modify templates and galleries
details: Store custom templates and brand elements that survive updates
Three-tier customization hierarchy supports team and organization settings
Built-in backup and restore prevents loss of customizations

entries.headline: RevOps assessments now automatically detect your revenue fields
details: Identifies custom revenue fields like Net New ARR and Annual Contract Value
Segments sales processes based on your org's configuration
Adapts analysis to your specific revenue tracking setup

entries.headline: Windows users now get Git Bash instead of Linux subsystem
Setup process now installs required packages automatically
Added PowerShell setup option for Windows users
Improved setup experience and plugin detection reliability
New tools prevent problematic Salesforce deployments
details: Validates picklist and record type configurations before deployment
Checks field history tracking limits to prevent failures
Merges duplicate settings automatically to avoid conflicts

entries.headline: Added automatic secret scanning to prevent credential leaks
Implemented secret scanning workflow for continuous security monitoring
Removed embedded credentials from code for better security
